title: Local obituary: Elizabeth ‘Lizzie’ Dankert, 24, Central Catholic and Union College alum
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: Elizabeth 'Lizzie' Dankert, 24, a Central Catholic and Union College alum, passed away leaving a lasting impact on her community.
Lizzie was a dedicated soccer player, English major, and writing tutor at Union College, cherished by family and friends.
Funeral services for Lizzie will be held May 21, 2026, at St. Augustine’s Church, Andover, with calling hours on May 20.

title: Don’t mess with the nest: Goose expert responds to viral Boston goose attack video
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A viral video shows a goose attacking a dog in Boston, with 6.7 million views and over 925,700 likes.
Goose researcher Mikaela Higgs explains the bird was acting defensively to protect its young during nesting season.
Higgs advises giving nesting geese extra space in urban areas and highlights they are accustomed to human activity.

title: Jackson Mann site could include housing, study finds ahead of community meeting
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: A study suggests housing can be added to the Jackson Mann Community Center site in Allston, according to Mayor Wu.
A community meeting on May 28 will discuss the potential redevelopment of Jackson Mann, including housing separate from the center.
The city's $10 million allocation for Jackson Mann covers design and permitting, with overall funding to increase as plans progress.

title: Here’s how hot meteorologists say it could get in Boston this week
author: Carson Lyle and Heather Alterisio
bullets: Heat Advisory issued from 11AM Tuesday to 8PM Wednesday for Boston due to record high temperatures.
Temperatures expected to peak in the mid-90s, challenging records, before cooling on Thursday.
There is a risk of afternoon thunderstorms Tuesday and Wednesday amidst the heat wave.

title: Here’s what Patriots could reportedly net in a Kayshon Boutte trade
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Kayshon Boutte may leave Patriots due to potential arrival of A.J. Brown next month.
Boutte hasn't joined Patriots' offseason workouts; reportedly working out independently instead.
Patriots could trade Boutte for a fifth- or sixth-round draft pick, according to Sports Illustrated.

title: Fundraisers launched as 8 remain hospitalized days after fire, explosion at Maine lumber mill
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Eight remain hospitalized following a fire and explosion at Robbins Lumber mill, which killed 27-year-old firefighter Andrew Cross.
Community fundraisers have raised over $30,000 towards a $40,000 goal to support injured first responders and victims.
State investigators and the ATF are examining the site; Robbins Lumber is cooperating and operating portions of the mill safely.

title: Shams Charania expects Celtics’ pursuit of Giannis Antetokounmpo to carry into ‘this offseason’
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Celtics have shown interest in acquiring Giannis Antetokounmpo from Milwaukee Bucks this offseason.
Milwaukee Bucks are open to trade discussions as they retool their roster following years of underperformance.
Other teams interested in Antetokounmpo include the Timberwolves, Heat, Lakers, Cavaliers, and Knicks.

title: T police: Man’s throat slashed in bus station altercation
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A man's throat was slashed during an altercation at Roxbury's MBTA Nubian Square bus station, police report.
The victim suffered a non-life threatening neck injury and was taken to a local hospital by Boston EMS.
The weapon used in the assault is unclear, and the MBTA Transit Police are investigating the incident.

title: Red Sox get discouraging update on Roman Anthony’s injury after he tries swinging bat
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox's Roman Anthony experienced soreness while swinging, delaying his return from a sprained finger ligament.
Anthony, sidelined since May 4, initially hoped to return by May 15, but setbacks have pushed his timeline further.
In 30 games this season, Anthony has a .229/.354/.321 batting line with one home run and five RBIs.

title: Bill Belichick gets candid on Tom Brady’s growth with Patriots: ‘He wasn’t even really good, but he became great’
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bill Belichick discussed Tom Brady's rise from a fourth-string quarterback to a key player in the Patriots' dynasty.
Belichick highlighted Brady's commitment and gradual improvement leading to his role as a game changer by 2004.
Belichick also shared insights on his coaching experience at the University of North Carolina and disdain for coffee.
